Strong fairness/reciprocity and high institutional trust drive support for transparency, with libertarian privacy concerns only mildly tempering enthusiasm.
Fairness and reciprocity drive support, while low openness and moderate authoritarian submission create some resistance, resulting in moderate endorsement.

Strong GAL-TAN green orientation (82.32%), high pluralism support, and very high conscientiousness demand transparency, while populist anti-elitism (64.25%) reinforces the need to expose hidden influence.

High GAL-TAN pluralism and participatory democracy preferences, plus high internal efficacy, drive support for transparency, while moderate libertarian skepticism of state control and low authoritarianism keep the score from being extreme.

High internal political efficacy and a desire for order (conscientiousness) drive support for transparency, but moderate libertarian anti-censorship and low trust in government moderate this to a clear but not strong level.
